mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings and lower equity risk premiums over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in months with PAYEMS growth > 150k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate below 0.50% signals an ultra-accommodative monetary stance that reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as liquidity conditions remain supportive.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.26% as of 2012-08-15 indicates historical context of prior easing
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.50%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based demand strength across consumer and construction sectors, reducing equity risk premium and supporting forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to move in the same direction simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 370495.0 and HOUST at 1408.0 as of 2012-08-15
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion phase with synchronized consumer and housing activity
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns when both conditions are met do not exceed returns when only one condition is met (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index above 112 signals robust manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, indicating strong corporate earnings potential and positive forward equity returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
regime_hint: post-recession supply-side recovery with improving capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns when INDPRO > 112 are not significantly higher than when INDPRO ≤ 112 (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.8% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and increased recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand and growth expectations decline.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of periods when UNRATE > 4.8%, or Rank IC > -0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of rising inflation (CPI month-over-month growth > 0.3%) and elevated federal funds rate (> 2.0%) signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real purchasing power, producing negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 203.153 and FEDFUNDS at 5.26% as of 2012-08-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflationary pressures
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term > -0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns when both conditions are met do not underperform baseline returns (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (annualized quarterly change) below 1.5% signals late-cycle or recessionary demand weakness that reduces corporate profit growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11506.5 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2012-05-15
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of quarters when GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC > -0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in industrial production and payroll employment signals broad-based supply and labor capacity growth that supports sustained earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to show concurrent month-over-month growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.2% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.9282 and PAYEMS at 137410.0 as of 2012-08-15
regime_hint: synchronized supply-side and labor expansion in recovery phase
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns when both conditions are met do not exceed returns when only one condition is met (t-test p > 0.10).
